excerpt: All these types are sisters to each other, that is, they all work in the same way... Getting out of moderation is called suffering.
source_work: Risalat al-Mahiyyah al-Zaza wa al-Alam (KASHKOLE Binder 23, Ch. 59)
source_location_hint: Section 1 (id=996), introductory passage on pleasure and pain

excerpt: An example of this is that when the entire candle is burned, the fire then starts burning the candle. In the same way, when the heat of the stomach starts to work on itself, the person starts to suffer.
source_work: Risalat al-Mahiyyah al-Zaza wa al-Alam (KASHKOLE Binder 23, Ch. 59)

excerpt: That is, after every sorrow, the system of pleasure and after every pleasure the system of pain has been set.
source_work: Risalat al-Mahiyyah al-Zaza wa al-Alam (KASHKOLE Binder 23, Ch. 59)
source_location_hint: Section 1, Food Enjoyment subsection, final paragraph

excerpt: When the animal moves a lot, the friction in the body organs causes heat and constipation in the temperament... Through sleep, the body's organs begin to cool down.
source_work: Risalat al-Mahiyyah al-Zaza wa al-Alam (KASHKOLE Binder 23, Ch. 59)
